Our guests this week are Joyce Karam, editor-in-chief of Al-Monitor, and Barry Malone, former Africa and Middle East correspondent for Reuters.

title: Gladio: NATO’s secret cold war operation in Italy
description: One of Europe’s biggest state secrets – and a scandal that still shakes Italy today – was a clandestine army set up by NATO after World War II to block the spread of communism. During the Cold War, cells of the network operated in around ten European countries. In Italy, the covert operation was known by the code name Gladio. Natalia Mendoza, Tommaso Marro, and Laura Roudaut report.

title: Ceasefire holds, but Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill

FRANCE 24 speaks to Adi Imsirovic, lecturer on the energy systems at the University of Oxford.

description: French President Emmanuel Macron is set to have his first face-to-face meeting with Pope Leo XIV in the Vatican Friday as the leader of the Roman Catholic church has criticised his fellow American, US President Donald Trump, for "unacceptable belligerence" during the Iran war. Macron has called for a negotiated end to the conflict in the Middle East.

title: Tyson Fury insists he’s ‘still got it’ ahead of Makhmudov comeback fight
description: While eyeing a fight with Anthony Joshua, Fury first faces a Russian-born heavyweight who wrestled a bear for fun.

title: Iran war: What is happening on day 42 of US-Israeli attacks?
description: A Pakistan-brokered US-Iran ceasefire has taken effect but disputes over its scope are fuelling regional tensions.

title: Middle East War Triggers Higher Prices in China
description: Three and a half years of deflationary pressure on Chinese factories reversed course last month as higher energy prices cycled into the economy.

title: Xi Seeks to Sway Taiwan, and Trump, With Message of Stability
description: Ahead of a summit with Donald Trump, the Chinese leader is using a rare meeting with a Taiwanese politician to cast Beijing as a peacemaker and squeeze the island’s president.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Strikes Hezbollah as Lebanon Impasse Threatens Cease-Fire
description: Israel said it would continue striking the Iran-backed militia. Iran said it would not attend peace talks with the United States in Pakistan if the truce was not extended to Lebanon.

title: No Longer Off Limits, the Strait of Hormuz Remains Thorny Politically
description: Even after a cease-fire, Iran is keeping a chokehold on traffic, forcing countries to cut deals that could put them at odds with the U.S.

title: Kuwait accuses Iran of continuing drone attacks despite ceasefire; Tehran blames Israel, US
description:
Hezbollah rocket hits home in Misgav Am, causing damage but no injuries * Jerusalem slams mediator Pakistan after its defense minister calls Israel ‘curse for humanity, cancerous’
The post Kuwait accuses Iran of continuing drone attacks despite ceasefire; Tehran blames Israel, US appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: US Republicans block attempt to rein in Trump’s war powers over Iran campaign
description:
US president's threat that 'a whole civilization will die' sparks new concern among Democrats, who have begun to openly float impeachment for the first time since his reelection
The post US Republicans block attempt to rein in Trumps war powers over Iran campaign appeared first on The Times of Israel.
